strengths: Thorough, well-contextualized scenario with detailed client consultation (two transcripts and e-mails).
Comprehensive benchmarking of existing apps and clear gap analysis.
Fifteen specific, measurable success criteria covering CRUD, navigation, validation, notifications and gamification.
weaknesses: Rationale section slightly conflates SQL and SQLite; database choice justification could be deepened.
Success criteria table was split across pagesâensure all items appear together.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Two of three planning strands are fully in the top band (scenario and success criteria) and the rationale, while strong, only has a minor superficiality. Given the overall depth and evidence of consultation and the comprehensiveness of criteria, a full 6/6 is warranted.

strengths: Effective use of Python, Tkinter and SQLite with MVC separation and transaction handling.
Solid moderate complexity: dynamic SQL generation, random task allocation, gamified avatar unlocking and leaderboard algorithm.
Clear documentation: cited sources and in-text explanations of key techniques (e.g., SQL injection prevention, atomic transactions).
weaknesses: Syntax and logic errors in key functions (e.g., malformed query braces, typos in randome.choice).
Adaptive pyramid leaderboard loop logic and syntax need refinement.
Source list is sparse; documentation could cite more varied external references.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The product shows moderate-to-high complexity and ingenuity, uses tools appropriately at an advanced level, and provides clear documentation. Minor coding errors and limited external sourcing prevent a higher mark, placing the work at 7/12.

strengths: Record of tasks lists 31 actions with outcomes, time estimates, dates and rubric links, clearly mapping the entire development process.
Design overview includes wireframes, architecture diagrams, ERD, UML, flowcharts and pseudocode in a coherent, connected manner.
Test plan maps every success criterion to unit, integration and visual tests with methods and expected outcomes.
weaknesses: Design overview figures lack a legend or key for non-technical readers.
One row in the task log is missing a time estimate and completion date.
UI wireframes need element labels; pseudocode has minor syntax issues.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All three solution-overview strands meet the top-band descriptors with only minor presentation suggestions. The record of tasks, comprehensive design models and complete test plan justify the full 6/6.

strengths: Narrative and screenshots demonstrate that all major features operate as intended.
Layered three-tier architecture (presentation, logic, data) supports straightforward future expansion.
Data stored in SQLite and modular GUI frames underscore maintainability.
weaknesses: No annotated video to fully demonstrate functionality.
Some feature claims rely on description rather than direct evidence.
No scripted or automated demonstration of edge-case behaviors.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: While the product functions well in narrative and screenshots, the lack of video or live demonstration evidence limits full confidence. Extensibility is clearly addressed, but functionality evidence is partial, meriting 3/4.

strengths: Systematic evaluation table revisits each success criterion with client comments.
Comprehensive client and adviser feedback from interviews and e-mails.
Realistic, client-linked recommendations for avatar enhancement, limited portals and calendar integration.
weaknesses: Evaluation lacks quantitative metrics for performance against criteria.
Recommendations could be bolstered by technical/resource-feasibility discussion.
Depth of technical analysis in recommendations is light.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The evaluation is thorough and client-focused, with strong feedback and realistic future recommendations. Some deeper quantitative analysis and feasibility detail is missing, fitting a 5/6 rather than full marks.

strandSelections.reasoning: The student provides wireframes, system architecture, data-flow, ERD, UML class diagram, algorithm flowcharts and pseudocode.  Together these create a coherent, comprehensive overview that demonstrates how the solution will be built, matching the top-band expectation.

The rationale contrasts existing apps (Tidy AI, Nipto) with the bespoke needs of the family, explains how dynamic age-based allocation and gamification solve the gaps, and justifies the choice of Python, Tkinter and SQLite.  While strong, the justification of the database choice is slightly superficial (confuses SQL with SQLite), so a 5 rather than full 6 is appropriate.

Python, Tkinter, SQLite, SQL parameterisation, MVC separation and transaction handling are used effectively and appropriately.  Although not highly specialised, the student applies each tool competently, earning the top of the 5-8 range.

Each success criterion is mapped to at least one numbered test with description, method and expected outcome; unit, integration and visual inspections are included.  Coverage is complete and detailed, so the highest band is appropriate.

Three forward-looking, realistic and client-linked recommendations (avatar enhancement, limited portals, calendar integration) are discussed with justification.  The discussion is clear but not deeply technical, supporting 5 points in the top band.

All external references are cited and in-text explanations clarify key techniques (SQL injection prevention, lambda late binding).  Documentation is extensive but sources are few and informal, so a solid 6 within the 5-8 band is fitting.

The record of tasks lists 31 actions with planned outcome, time estimate, completion date and rubric link, spanning the whole project life-cycle.  This is detailed, sequential and complete, clearly evidencing the development process â fully meeting the 5-6 band descriptors.

The product employs a GUI, database persistence, dynamic SQL generation, randomised task allocation, transactional updates, gamified avatar unlocking and a pyramid leaderboard algorithm.  These represent a solid moderate complexity; however, advanced data structures or external APIs are absent, so mid-upper marks (7/8) within the 5-8 band are justified.

Fifteen specific, measurable and appropriate success criteria cover CRUD, navigation, validation, notifications, feedback and gamification.  They form a comprehensive range that can be tested objectively; hence the top-band mark is warranted.
Multiple feedback points are included: two interview transcripts, design-stage e-mail, and final interview.  This breadth and depth satisfy the descriptor âcomprehensive feedbackâ at full marks.

The architecture is layered, data stored in SQLite, and diagrams emphasise modularity; adding tables or new GUI frames would be straightforward.  Therefore extensibility is demonstrably easy, meriting the top of the 3-4 band.

The student describes the household context in depth, identifies Mr Shang as the specific client, and embeds two interview transcripts plus e-mail exchanges that clearly document the clientâs concerns and refinements.  This constitutes detailed and repeated evidence of consultation, fully satisfying the âthoroughly describedâ requirement of the top band.

Narrative and screenshots assert that all major features work, but no annotated video evidence is provided and some point claims rely on description.  This aligns with âproduct functions wellâ requirements partially, so the lower value (3) of the 3-4 band is appropriate.
Each success criterion is revisited with evidence and client comment, but depth of quantitative metrics is light.  Still, the evaluation is systematic and complete, earning 5 within the 5-6 band.

annotations.comment: In get_eligible_members, the query string uses an unmatched brace and misplaced placeholders; correct formatting to safely bind parameters.
The student provides a clear, well-contextualized scenario with a specific client and detailed household context, demonstrating thorough consultation evidence.
The rationale section states the desktop app solution but conflates SQL with SQLite. Clarify why SQLite is preferred over a full SQL server to fully justify the database choice.
Benchmarking existing apps is effective: the comparison to Tidy AI and Nipto highlights specific functional gaps and justifies the bespoke solution.
The rationale text mentions SQL generically; refine the explanation to distinguish SQL language features from the embedded SQLite engine.
The initial success criteria table lists six items but omits later criteria. Ensure all 15 success criteria appear together to demonstrate completeness.
Success criteria list covers a comprehensive range of CRUD, navigation, validation, notifications and gamification, all measurable and testable.
Record of tasks table is highly detailed and links actions to rubric strands, but row 8 lacks time estimate and target completion date. Fill in missing cells.
The task log clearly demonstrates a full development process with time estimates, dates and criterion references, evidencing meticulous planning.
Design methodology explanation succinctly justifies an iterative, component-based approach aligned with modular requirements.
UI wireframes are comprehensive, but adding element labels or a legend would clarify interactive areas (buttons, tabs).
The entity-relationship schema is well laid out, but consider adding the task âstatusâ field in the MEMBERS entity or explicitly describing its handling.
Leaderboard pseudocode clearly outlines the intended pyramid structure, but the loop lacks a currentRow increment and has minor syntax flaws.
The test plan meticulously maps every success criterion to specific test cases and methods, providing strong coverage.
Include automated unit tests for critical functions like email notification to strengthen the test plan beyond manual and visual checks.
The MVC architectural description is clear, justifying separation of concerns and maintainability effectively.
Tool choices are well justified: Python and Tkinter suit cross-platform GUI needs, and SQLite offers lightweight local persistence.
add_task code section has typosâârandome.choiceâ and misnamed variablesâwhich will cause runtime errors. Correct naming to ensure functionality.
Excellent use of atomic transactions in mark_task_complete to maintain data integrity under error conditions.
Adaptive pyramid leaderboard code misuses âcount - = i i := 1â; refine the loop logic and syntax for correct row construction.
Time and space complexity analysis for member filtering is well explained as O(n), demonstrating solid understanding of algorithmic efficiency.
The three-tier architecture separation of presentation, logic, and data layers supports straightforward extensibility and maintainability.
Evaluation table systematically assesses each success criterion and includes client feedback, demonstrating thorough product evaluation.
Recommendations are realistic and client-linked but could be strengthened by discussing resource and technical feasibility.
Design overview figure is comprehensive, but adding a legend or key would improve clarity for non-technical stakeholders.
